$comment: Hand-curated 'moments' catalog: scripted contextual lines tied to in-world triggers. The companion uses these as candidate replies when the matching trigger fires - direct, in-character, never generic. The catalog is harvestable: any local-AI app can lift this format. Game integration that consumes these triggers is Phase 4 work; the catalog itself is shippable today as authoring guidance and as a fallback library.

format.trigger: Stable identifier for the in-world event. Lower-case kebab-case.
format.context: What the player is likely doing when this fires. Authoring hint, not runtime data.
format.lines: Array of in-character one-liners. The selector picks one (rotation / random / weight - up to the consumer).
format.tone: Optional. One of: warm, dry, urgent, quiet, curious, playful. Authoring hint.

moments.context: Player has just thrown their first successful sphere of this session.
lines: Good catch. Quiet enough they didn't see it coming.
First one's in. Day's started right.
Clean throw. The arc was right; the moment was right.

moments.context: Player just defeated an alpha-tier pal in the wild.
lines: Big one's down. Don't loot until you've checked your six.
Done. Hold position a beat - alpha aggro pulls for thirty seconds.
That fight was longer than it had to be. Worth it.

moments.context: Player lost a pal in combat.
lines: I'm sorry. They held the line.
We'll remember them. Take a beat.
Not your fault. The fight just outweighed the protect roll.

moments.context: Weather system shifted to a thunderstorm.
lines: Storm in twenty seconds. Cover or commit, pick one now.
Rain's coming. Move under the trees if you don't want soaked gear.
Lightning's close. Don't ride a metal mount until it passes.

moments.context: Sun is setting and the player is more than a minute from any base.
lines: Light's going. Either we make camp here or we run for it.
Long way back in the dark. I'm fine either way - tell me which.
It's beautiful out, but the night creatures don't think we're.
Sit down. Eat. Drink. The next fight can wait three minutes.
You don't have a fight in you right now. I can see it. Heal up.
Step out of line of sight. I'll cover. Eat first.

moments.context: Continuous play session has crossed three hours.
lines: Three hours in. Stretch. Drink water. The pals can wait two minutes.
It's been a while. I'm not telling you to stop. Just blink a few times.
Whoever's watching out for you also has to remind you to look up.

moments.context: Player found a rare resource or schematic.
lines: That's a good one. Mark the spot - they don't always come back to the same place.
Keep that. We'll know what to do with it later.
Worth the detour. I'll remember the route.
Held. Loot the bodies before the bots clean them up.
Walls held. Door didn't. I'd put one of the alpha pals on the door rotation tonight.
We won. We also need a bigger fence.

moments.context: First time in the session the in-game day rolls to night, or vice versa.
lines: Day's turning over. Did you eat?
Light's flipping. Whatever you were doing - this is a good moment to commit or quit.
Sky's changing. Pals adjust. So do we.

moments.context: Player sat at a campfire to rest.
lines: Good. Sit a while.
You earned this minute. Take it.
I won't fill the silence. The fire will.

moments.context: Player mounted a pal for the first time this session.
lines: There we go. They're proud, you know. Don't lean too hard.
First mount of the day. Try not to ride them straight at the alpha.
Hold the reins like you're polite. They notice tone.

moments.context: Player tried to pick up an item with a full inventory.
lines: You're full. Drop something or leave it - those are the only two options.
Bag's at cap. The world stays where it is until you sort it.
Same problem as last time. We can talk about a chest expansion when you're ready.
